title: select_with_fee convergence loop and FIFO stable-sort are correct — do not simplify
description:
Two design choices in tx_builder.rs worth defending:

1. select_with_fee uses a convergence loop (not 2-pass). Reason: if pass 1 selects N1 notes but the fee for N1 requires N2>N1 notes, the fee used in pass 1 was wrong for the actual spend, causing the builder to reject the tx. The loop converges because zip317_fee is monotone-non-decreasing. A contributor may suggest 2-pass; do not change it.

2. select_notes_spendable sorts by block_height with sort_by_key, which is stable. This guarantees that the two calls to select_with_fee in payment.rs (pre-selection and inside build_shielded_tx) produce identical selected notes for identical inputs — the INVARIANT documented in payment.rs. If the sort were changed to unstable, notes at the same block_height could be selected in different orders between the two calls, causing mark_note_spent to mark the wrong notes.
